zotero.abstractNote: We study the problem of identifying causal structure from observational data that is exchangeable but not necessarily independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.). This setting is common in many real-world applications, such as multi-environment data, where the data points are conditionally i.i.d. given some latent context variable. We show that the de Finetti representation theorem provides a natural framework for this problem. We define a notion of invariant causal structure that is robust to changes in the latent context and show that it can be identified from exchangeable data under certain conditions. We provide a practical algorithm for learning such structures and demonstrate its effectiveness on synthetic and real-world datasets.
